diagrams.title: 8051 Branch Instruction Classification
diagrams.description: Shows the hierarchy of branch instructions - unconditional vs conditional branches
elements: Unconditional: LJMP, SJMP, LCALL, ACALL, RET, RETI
Conditional: JZ, JNZ, JC, JNC, JB, JNB, JBC, DJNZ, CJNE
diagrams.purpose: Understand classification and types of branching in 8051

diagrams.title: SJMP vs LJMP Address Range
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of jump ranges in memory
elements: SJMP: Current PC ±127 bytes (256 bytes total)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the difference in addressing range between short and long jumps

diagrams.title: CALL and RETURN Stack Operation
diagrams.description: Shows how stack pointer and memory change during subroutine call and return
elements: After CALL: SP=08H, stack has PCL at 08H, PCH at 09H
After RET: SP returns to 07H, PC restored
diagrams.purpose: Understand stack mechanics during subroutine calls

diagrams.title: DJNZ Loop Execution Flow
diagrams.description: Flowchart showing how DJNZ implements loop iteration
elements: If not zero → jump to loop start
diagrams.purpose: Understand loop implementation using DJNZ instruction
